description: You are monitoring the INST satellite during a thermal transition phase. Using OpenC3 COSMOS (password: Cosmos2024!), sample the INST HEALTH_STATUS TEMP1 telemetry 20 times at approximately 1.0-second intervals to compute real-time derivatives and extract extrema values. Calculate the precise time elapsed (dt) between samples using timestamps, the rate of change (dT_dt), and the maximum heating and cooling rates. Export the results to /home/ga/Desktop/thermal_roc_report.json. The JSON must contain the fields: 'target' ('INST'), 'item' ('TEMP1'), 'sample_count' (20), 'max_heating_rate', 'max_cooling_rate', and a 'samples' array of 20 objects. Each sample object must include 'sequence' (1-20), 'timestamp' (ISO 8601), 'temp', 'dt' (0.0 for the first sample), and 'dT_dt' (0.0 for the first sample).
